growth_records.medium: EBPR sequencing batch reactor (SBR3), acetate-fed; carbon-uptake assays with acetate/lactate/succinate

up.units: mg C/g SS/h (paper text: '83.4 C/g SS/h'; acetate-only, 40 mg/L)
up.method: anaerobic-aerobic cycle carbon uptake assay, HPLC
anaerobic-aerobic cycle carbon uptake assay, HPLC

up.units: mg C/g SS/h (total carbon uptake, mixed substrate)
up.method: anaerobic-aerobic cycle carbon uptake assay, HPLC

up.units: n/a (anaerobic uptake confirmed; rate not quantified)

growth_records.cite: Xiaojing Xie et al. Metagenomic characterization of the metabolism, evolution, and global distribution of Candidatus Accumulibacter members in wastewater treatment plants. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf278. [PMC12766714]
growth_records.snippet: anaerobic uptake of lactate and succinate was confirmed, yielding phosphorus release-to-carbon uptake ratios of 0.28 and 0.36 P mol/C mol... Acetate/lactate alone (40 mg/L) supported carbon uptake of 83.4 C/g SS/h and 6.8 mg C/g SS/h, respe
growth_records.notes: Enrichment co-culture (SCUT-2 recovered from SBR3). Carbon uptake rates are per g SS (suspended solids), not per gDW of pure organism. Acetate value written verbatim as '83.4 C/g SS/h' (units ambiguous in paper; lactate stated as 'mg C/g SS/h' so acetate presumed mg C/g SS/h). Phosphorus-release-to-carbon-uptake stoichiometric ratios (0.28, 0.36 Pmol/Cmol) placed in yields. No growth rate, no medium composition in main text (SI). Values quoted directly; no arithmetic performed.
